nodes.description: Jonathan explained that God's forgiveness is not based on deserving it but on His love, stating, 'He doesn't forgive us because we deserve it, but because he loves us. And he's made a way for us to be forgiven, through Jesus.'

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Jonathan shared his own mistakes and the importance of forgiveness
nodes.description: Jonathan recounted his own mistakes, including shoplifting and cheating, emphasizing that forgiveness is not about being good enough but about accepting Jesus' sacrifice.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Jonathan emphasized that forgiveness includes thoughts, not just actions
nodes.description: Jonathan explained that forgiveness is about the condition of our hearts, referencing Jesus' teachings about thoughts leading to sin, saying, 'It's not just about our actions, Damian. It's about the condition of our hearts.'

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Damian expressed uncertainty about accepting faith
nodes.description: Damian conveyed his uncertainty about faith, stating, 'I think I understand what you're saying. But what if I'm still not sure about all this?'

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Jonathan reassured Damian of God's love and support
nodes.description: Jonathan reassured Damian that he is not alone and that there is hope for everyone, saying, 'I'm not here to force you to believe anything. I just want you to know that you're not alone, and that there is hope for everyone, no matter what they've done.'

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Religious ideologies and goals were the main driving forces behind the Crusades
nodes.description: The primary motivation for the Crusades was rooted in the Christian belief that holy sites were taken by Muslims, creating a sense of religious duty among Christian knights and warriors.

nodes.description: In addition to religious motivations, political and personal interests influenced the Crusades, including the Pope's desire to extend influence, nobles seeking territory and wealth, and knights pursuing adventure and glory.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Some leaders of the Crusades were more interested in conquering land than in religious goals
nodes.description: Certain leaders prioritized territorial conquest over the religious objectives of the Crusades, indicating a shift in focus from spiritual to material gains.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Many Crusaders were motivated by personal gain and loot rather than religious duty
nodes.description: The motivations of many Crusaders included personal gain and the pursuit of loot, overshadowing the original religious intentions of the Crusades.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Atrocities committed during the Crusades suggest that religious aspects may have been secondary to political and personal interests
nodes.description: The massacre of innocent civilians and other violent acts during the Crusades indicate that political and personal motivations may have taken precedence over religious goals.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Moral standards have shifted since the time of the Crusades

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Practical completion is a significant milestone in the construction industry
nodes.description: Practical completion signals the end of the construction process and indicates when the works under the contract have been completed to the extent that the owner can occupy and use the building for its intended purpose.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Practical completion determines when the defects liability period starts for property developers
nodes.description: Practical completion marks the beginning of the defects liability period, when the builder is liable to pay liquidated damages and when the builder receives final payment.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Practical completion triggers the commencement of the statutory warranty period under the Home Building Act 1989
nodes.description: Reaching practical completion initiates the statutory warranty period and insurance under the Home Building Compensation Fund (HBCF), which protects property developers against defects in their building work.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Consequences of failing to meet practical completion include financial penalties for property developers
nodes.description: Failing to meet practical completion can result in the builder claiming liquidated damages for delays, delaying the commencement of warranty and insurance coverage, and potentially leading to contract termination.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] User received practical tips for ensuring practical completion is met
nodes.description: Tips include engaging a reputable builder, ensuring contracts are in order, regularly inspecting work, addressing defects promptly, planning for contingencies, maintaining communication with the builder, and obtaining necessary inspections and approvals.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Practical completion is crucial for property developers to minimize risks and liabilities
nodes.description: Reaching practical completion helps mitigate risks and liabilities that developers face in building projects, transferring the risk of insuring the works to the developer.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] User concluded that practical completion is essential for timely project completion
nodes.description: The conclusion emphasizes the importance of practical completion for property developers, highlighting its role in triggering the defects liability period and insurance coverage, and the need to reach this milestone on time.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Data2vec 2.0 uses an inverse block masking strategy to preserve contiguous regions of samples
nodes.description: The inverse block masking strategy in Data2vec 2.0 is designed to preserve contiguous regions of the sample, thereby providing more context for student predictions.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Training targets in Data2vec 2.0 are based on averaging top K FFN blocks of the teacher
nodes.description: The training targets utilized in Data2vec 2.0 are derived from averaging the top K feedforward network (FFN) blocks of the teacher model.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Teacher weights in Data2vec 2.0 are an exponentially moving average of student encoder weights
nodes.description: In Data2vec 2.0, the weights of the teacher model are calculated as an exponentially moving average of the weights from the student encoder.

nodes.title: [2023-05-20] Data2vec 2.0 features an asymmetric encoder/decoder architecture
nodes.description: The architecture of Data2vec 2.0 is characterized by an asymmetric encoder/decoder structure, where the student encoder only encodes unmasked patches or time-steps.
